beats.activity: Wakes up, showers, makes pour-over coffee, checks bike inventory mentally.
beats.reasoning: Kwame starts his day at home with full energy. As someone who refuses drip coffee, he takes time for a proper pour-over. Monday morning, sunny weather puts him in a good headspace.

beats.activity: Drives to Redmond bike shop via I-5, arriving before the morning rush.
beats.reasoning: Kwame commutes early to beat traffic and get his workspace organized. The Light Rail closure between Westlake and U-District doesn't affect his route via I-5 north.

beats.activity: Works at bike shop: repairs, tune-ups, customer consultations.
beats.reasoning: Morning shift focuses on mechanical work before the FIFA match airs. Kwame is blunt and direct with customers, efficient with repairs. Introverted, so he prefers the focused work to small talk.

beats.activity: Lunch break: grabs a sandwich and espresso at local café, skips the FIFA match.
beats.reasoning: The USA vs Wales match airs at 11am, but Kwame is introverted and not a big sports fan. He values quiet focus and close friends over crowds at bars. Takes a solo lunch instead. No drip coffee—espresso is acceptable.

beats.activity: Afternoon work shift: more repairs, inventory management, mentoring junior mechanic.
beats.reasoning: Continues his Monday shift. Kwame is direct and blunt, so he gives clear feedback to junior staff. Afternoon stays busy as Monday cyclist commuters bring in weekend wear-and-tear bikes.

beats.activity: Drives home via I-5, enjoying the sunny evening light.
beats.reasoning: Evening commute home. High of 74F and sunny—perfect weather. Kwame plans his days around weather and mood, so he's likely thinking about evening activities. Light Rail closure is irrelevant to his I-5 route.

beats.activity: Quick errand: stops at local grocery for ingredients (hops, grains for home brewing).
beats.reasoning: Kwame spends free time on home brewing. On a sunny Monday evening, he picks up supplies for a weekend project. Stays local in his Ballard neighborhood—quick, focused trip.

beats.activity: Home: preps brewing equipment, sets up a batch, writes notes in brewing log.; Evening: writes and practices open-mic poetry, drafts new material.; Wind-down: reads, makes decaf tea, prepares for bed.
beats.reasoning: Kwame is already home. He channels his introverted energy into a solitary hobby—home brewing. Methodical, hands-on work that aligns with his blunt, direct personality and craftsmanship.

beats.activity: Sleeps soundly after a productive day.
beats.reasoning: 8 hours of sleep, ending at 6:30am next morning. Kwame starts the day with full energy and has a grounded, routine-oriented personality, so he gets solid rest before another work week begins.

diary: Monday mornings have a rhythm to them—pour-over coffee, mental inventory of what needs fixing at the shop. The drive to Redmond was smooth; I-5 cooperated for once. Spent the morning on the usual: a derailleur adjustment, two flat repairs, and a customer who'd been riding on dry-rotted tires for months. Grabbed a sandwich at the café instead of joining the FIFA crowd downtown. The younger guys were buzzing about it, but I wasn't feeling it today. Afternoon flew by—mentored Tyler on brake cable routing, caught up on inventory. The drive home was pure gold: that slant of June light hitting the water, the kind that makes you remember why you stay in Seattle. Picked up supplies for a new batch at Ballard Market, and by evening I had the kettle going, grains steeping. Spent time with my brewing log, then shifted gears entirely—worked on some poetry pieces for next week's open mic. There's something about switching from wrenches to words that settles me.
thought_process: I skipped the World Cup thing deliberately; everyone was going to be glued to screens, and I needed the space today. The Light Rail closure didn't affect my route, which felt like a small win. Work was solid—routine but satisfying, especially watching Tyler catch on to the cable routing. By afternoon my mind was already pivoting toward home, toward the brewing project I've been planning. The poetry is newer for me, something I picked up last year, and it's become the counterbalance to the mechanical work. One is about precision and function; the other is about feeling and ambiguity. Both matter. The sunny weather lifted something in me—there was no heaviness today, just motion and purpose.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
